facts.text: Central Square’s historic depot, once a joint station for the New York, Ontario & Western and New York Central railroads, burned in 1908 and was rebuilt the next year.
Richard Montgomery, a Revolutionary War soldier, settled in Central Square in the 1770s and his cottage still stands on Livingston Street today.
The Wilderstein mansion, a Hudson River landmark, showcases colonial architecture and archaeological finds dating back to 1853.
The name “Wilderstein” comes from a local petroglyph meaning “wild man’s stone,” a reminder of the Sepasco people who once lived here.
Rhinecliff, just two miles west of Central Square, hosts an Amtrak station that connects the village to the broader New York metropolitan area.
